normalized_question_text:
In an N-bit flash ADC, the analog voltage is fed simultaneously to \(2^N - 1\) comparators. The output of the comparators is then encoded to a binary format using digital circuits. Assume that the analog voltage source \(V_{\text{in}}\) (whose output is being converted to digital format) has a source resistance of 75 \(\Omega\), and the input capacitance of each comparator is 8 pF, as shown in the circuit diagram. The input must settle to an accuracy of 1/2 LSB even for a full-scale input change for proper conversion. Assume that the time taken by the thermometer-to-binary encoder is negligible.

If the flash ADC has 8-bit resolution, which one of the following alternatives is closest to the maximum sampling rate?
